learning_objective: Students will calculate the net energy absorbed by different surfaces and predict how albedo changes affect local temperature.
specification:
Type: microsim **sim-id:** solar-albedo
**Library:** p5.js
**Status:** Specified **Bloom Level:** Apply **Bloom Verb:** Calculate **Learning Objective:** Students will calculate the net energy absorbed by different surfaces and predict how albedo changes affect local temperature. **Instructional Rationale:** Manipulating surface types and seeing real-time energy budgets makes the abstract concept of albedo tangible and connects it to climate feedback loops. Visual: Side view of Earth's surface with incoming solar arrows from above. A dropdown or row of clickable surface types (snow, ocean, forest, desert, city, ice) changes the ground surface. Reflected arrows bounce off the surface proportional to albedo -- more arrows reflected for high-albedo surfaces. A temperature gauge on the right shows surface temperature rising or falling based on net absorbed energy. Below, a numerical display shows: incoming radiation, reflected radiation, absorbed radiation, and albedo value. A "Climate Feedback" toggle shows how changing from ice to ocean creates a positive feedback loop, with an animated cycle diagram. Color: incoming arrows yellow, reflected arrows white, absorbed shown as red glow on surface.
